# dropply-web usage guide for AI agents

`dropply-web` is the browser client of an end-to-end-encrypted file/text sharing
pair. All encryption and decryption happen **in the tab** (via `@cdlab/cipher`);
the 256-bit key travels only in the URL fragment (`#key=…`), which browsers never
send to a server. It is a Next.js App Router app **statically exported**
(`output: 'export'`) — no server of its own — and talks to a single backend,
`dropply-api`, which stores only ciphertext + metadata.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- HTTP client (all endpoints): ./src/lib/api.ts
- Crypto boundary: ./src/lib/crypto.ts

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web <script>`.

## What it is

- **Static export, no backend here.** `next.config.ts` `output: 'export'` →
  `next build` emits static HTML/JS to `./out`. No SSR, no route handlers, no
  edge/node functions. `src/middleware.ts` (next-intl) and `generateMetadata`
  run at dev/build only. All logic is client-side React.
- **Single-page, two tabs.** `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` (`'use client'`) is the
  whole UI: Share (encrypt + upload) and Retrieve (download + decrypt).
- **Depends entirely on `dropply-api`** — storage, TOTP verification, and email
  live there. The base URL is `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (build-time inlined).

## Crypto boundary (src/lib/crypto.ts)

- `generateEncryptionKey()` — 32 random bytes → base64url no-padding; used as the
  Argon2id *password*, not a raw key.
- Key rides the URL **fragment**: `encodeKeyForUrl` / `decodeKeyFromUrl` parse
  `key=` from the hash. Share URL: `…?code=<retrievalCode>#key=<key>`. Code in the
  query (server-visible), key in the fragment (never transmitted).
- `encryptFile` / `decryptFile` (streaming), `encryptTextContent` /
  `decryptTextContent` (base64) wrap `@cdlab/cipher`. Files re-wrapped as
  `application/octet-stream` so ciphertext MIME leaks nothing.

## Share pipeline (src/lib/api.ts `uploadContent`, hook `usePocketChest`)

1. `getConfig()` → `{ requireTOTP, emailShareEnabled, maxFileSize }` (on mount).
2. TOTP gate if `requireTOTP` and no token — validated by `createChest(token)`,
   there is NO dedicated verify endpoint.
3. `createChest(totpToken?)` → `{ sessionId, uploadToken }`.
4. Encrypt EACH file/text item in-browser. Encryption = the FIRST 50% of a
   file's progress bar; then `totalBytes` is replaced with the ciphertext size.
5. Driver split by size (`CHUNK_SIZE = 20MB`, computed from encrypted sizes):
   - text → `uploadContentRegular` (one FormData) FIRST.
   - small ≤20MB → rolling pool, `MAX_CONCURRENT_SMALL_FILES = 3` parallel.
   - large >20MB → SEQUENTIAL per file; each file multipart, `min(3, totalParts)`
     parts per batch, `MAX_RETRIES = 3` (linear `1000ms × attempt` backoff).
6. `completeUpload(...)` → `{ retrievalCode, expiryDate }`.
7. Build URL; push `{ id, retrievalCode, encryptionKey, shareUrl, timestamp }` to
   `useShareStore` (id from `@cdlab/driftflake` `genid`).

Key invariants (do not break without reading DESIGN §3, §4, §8):

- The `uploadId` returned by `createMultipartUpload` is itself a JWT
  (`multipartToken`), DISTINCT from the chest `uploadToken`. Parts + complete use
  the multipart token; create + regular upload use the chest token.
- Two concurrency layers: 3 files (small) OR 3 parts of ONE file (large) — large
  files never upload in parallel with each other.
- `XMLHttpRequest` is used ONLY when an `onProgress` callback is present (fetch
  can't report upload progress); `fetch` is the no-progress fallback.

## Retrieve pipeline (usePocketChest `retrieve` / `downloadSingleFile`)

- `page.tsx` reads `?code=` (from `useSearchParams`) and `#key=` (synchronously
  from `window.location.hash` in a `useState` initializer — the fragment isn't in
  searchParams). A present code makes the initial tab `retrieve`.
- `retrieveChest(code)` → `{ files, chestToken, expiryDate }`. Text files
  decrypted EAGERLY inline; binary files decrypted LAZILY per download.
- All `/api/download/:fileId` calls carry `Authorization: Bearer <chestToken>`.

## Client storage (src/store/*, src/lib/storage.ts)

- `useShareStore` — `localStorage` `dropply-share-results`. `partialize` REWRITES
  `encryptionKey` to `''` before persisting — the key is NEVER on disk. Do not
  change this; it is the threat model.
- `useRetrieveStore` — `localStorage` `dropply-retrieve-results`. `partialize`
  strips text `content`; bodies go to IndexedDB (`dbStore`, key
  `${resultId}:${fileId}`). `onRehydrateStorage` → `rehydrateTextContents()` sets
  `isHydrated` (errors STILL set it true to avoid deadlock).
- `useAuthStore` — `{ totpToken }` in `sessionStorage` `dropply-auth`
  (session-scoped, not durable).
- No backend DB in this app. API type contracts: `src/types/index.ts`.

## Config & endpoints

- Only build-time env: `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (`src/lib/api.ts`, default `''` =
  same-origin relative `/api`, served by nsl in dev; `.env.example` points at a
  Worker URL for production). Inlined into the static bundle — a change needs a
  rebuild.
- Runtime config from `GET /api/config` (`requireTOTP`, `emailShareEnabled`,
  `maxFileSize`; client default cap `100MB`). No rebuild to change server config.
- No Cloudflare bindings (no KV/R2/D1/DO). `wrangler.jsonc` serves `./out` as
  static assets only.
- Backend endpoints (all via `PocketChestAPI`, envelope `{ code, message, data }`,
  success `code === 0`): `/api/config`, `/api/chest`, `/api/chest/:id/upload`,
  `/api/chest/:id/multipart/create`, `/api/chest/:id/multipart/:fileId/part/:n`,
  `/api/chest/:id/multipart/:fileId/complete`, `/api/chest/:id/complete`,
  `/api/retrieve/:code`, `/api/download/:fileId`, `/api/email/share`.

## Gotchas for agents

- Zero-knowledge: NEVER send the key to the server; it belongs only in the URL
  fragment. NEVER persist `encryptionKey` to `localStorage` (the share-store
  partialize blanks it on purpose).
- Static export: there is NO server here. Don't add API routes, SSR fetching, or
  server components expecting a runtime. `middleware.ts` is inert in production.
- `nodejs_compat` is set in `wrangler.jsonc` for the `next-on-pages` path even
  though the app is static — do NOT assume Node APIs in the client bundle.
- The multipart `uploadId` is a JWT (`multipartToken`) — don't confuse it with the
  chest `uploadToken`.
- This app is useless without a reachable `dropply-api`; run/deploy one first.
- No test script and no test files exist — don't invent `pnpm … test`.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web dev        # http://dropply-web.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web lint       # next lint
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web typecheck  # tsc --noEmit
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web build       # next build → static out/
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web build:cf    # next-on-pages (Cloudflare Pages)
```
